key_insight: Masking order matters in masked diffusion language models, so the policy gradient should optimize both token choices and which positions to remask.
engineering_relevance: This gives a practical reinforcement-learning recipe for improving MDLM reasoning performance without relying on an exact log-likelihood estimate.
method: Formulate MDLM generation as a two-stage action MDP.
Decompose the policy gradient into a token term and a masking term.
Optimize both terms jointly during training.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports state-of-the-art results on mathematical reasoning and code benchmarks, including 87.1% on GSM8K and 53.4% on MBPP.
watch_item: The approach adds an extra masking-policy component, so training stability and implementation complexity may be higher than token-only RL methods.
paper_title: Mask-Aware Policy Gradients for Diffusion Language Models
